messages.content:
Story set in Supergirl tv show. Story about Lena Luthor & Nicolas. romance & sensual undertone
Nicolas description for references: 1m78, and a bit overweight, short skipy brown hairs & brown eyes behind glasses. He doesn’t grow past his shyness & awkward way with Lena. He is just a delivery guy new in town. Taking cooking class with Alex Danvers. 
Lena Luthor is more assertive in this story & don’t second guess on her desires. Lena finds refreshing that someone doesn’t seem to care about her family name . She will be the one initiating each step in their growing relationship. For future references: 1: Nicolas calls her « Miss Lena » until further notice. No sexual relations without further notice. 
Lena Luthor is finding herself in a journey to slowly seduce Nicolas. He is a breath of fresh hair for her even if he only manages to say « hello » and « have a good day to her ». Nicolas never get past his shy & akward demeanor with Lena . It is something she find alluring. Lena find herself curious about this man that doesn’t recognize her when he has a delivery for her. Write their fist meeting.

It was just a regular day at L-Corp when the doorbell rang. Lena Luthor had been going through paperwork and was eager for a break. She walked to the door and opened it, finding a delivery man standing on the other side.

"Hello, Miss Lena," he said, his voice shaky.

Lena was taken aback by the fact that he knew her name. Most people in National City did, of course, but she hadn't expected a simple delivery man to be aware of her identity.

"Hello," she replied, studying him more closely. He was short and a bit overweight, with short, messy brown hair and brown eyes behind glasses. There was something endearing about him that Lena couldn't quite put her finger on.

"Package for you," he said, holding out a small box.

"Thank you," Lena said, taking the box from him. "Is there anything else?"

"N-no, that's all," he stuttered.

"Alright then," Lena said, giving him a small smile. "Have a good day."

"You too, Miss Lena," he replied, starting to back away from the door.

Lena watched him go, feeling a sense of curiosity she hadn't felt in a while. Nicolas, as she would later learn his name was, may have been shy and awkward, but there was something about him that drew her in. It wasn't long before she found herself looking for excuses to order deliveries just to see him again. And Nicolas, for his part, was just as infatuated with her as she was with him. 

It started with shy glances and ended with long conversations about everything from his favorite books to her ambitions for L-Corp. Through it all, Lena enjoyed being the one in control, taking the lead in their growing relationship. And while they may not have crossed any physical boundaries yet, there was a sensual undertone to every look and touch between them. Lena had never felt more alive, and Nicolas was content just to bask in the glow of her attention.

Lena invite Nicolas for a date. Later that day, while Lena is talking about it with the Danvers sister, she notice Alex receiving a message from NIcolas. Grinning, Alex show the message to Kara & Lena "General Danvers, you’ve save my life during the cookie war & i’m again in need of your help. Please please please tell me how not to make a fool of myself. I’ve this date with someone called “Lena” and i’ve strictly no idea, i repeat, no idea, what i should do to avoid making this a disaster. helllllpppp ! pretty please ! Nico’ "

Lena chuckled at Nicolas' message, finding it endearing how nervous he was. "I'm sure you don't need to worry, Nicolas," she said, smiling at him.

"I don't know, Miss Lena," he replied, fiddling with his glasses nervously. "I want to make a good impression on you."

"You already have," Lena said, reaching out to take his hand in hers. "But if it will ease your nerves, I can give you a few tips."

Nicolas nodded eagerly, and Lena spent the next half hour detailing everything from what to wear to what topics to avoid. Nicolas listened intently, hanging on her every word.

After Lena was done, she walked out into the living room where she found the Danvers sisters chatting. "So, you're going on a date with Nicolas?" Kara asked, grinning.

"Yes, I am," Lena said, her eyes twinkling. "And it looks like he's already asking for advice from the cookie queen herself."

Alex pulled out her phone and showed Lena and Kara the message she'd just received from Nicolas. Lena laughed. "Tell him it's going to be okay," she said. "He's a sweet guy, and there's really nothing to worry about."

"Will do," Alex said, typing a message back to Nicolas. "And hey, if all else fails, just remember to be yourself."

Lena nodded, feeling a sense of satisfaction that she could put Nicolas' mind at ease. This was going to be a good date, she could feel it.
Lena & Nicolas are having a good time for their evening date.

Lena and Nicolas' date was off to a good start. They had reservations at a fancy restaurant in town, and Lena was pleased to see that Nicolas had taken her advice about what to wear. He looked handsome in a clean white shirt and black pants, and she found herself admiring him as they walked into the restaurant together.

Once they were seated, they perused the menu, making small talk as they decided what to order. Lena was pleased to see that Nicolas seemed more relaxed now, no longer the nervous delivery boy she'd met a few days ago.

As they ate, they talked about everything from work to their childhoods, and Lena found Nicolas to be quite charming. There was a sense of vulnerability about him that she found endearing, and she felt herself slipping into her role as the confident, assertive woman she'd always wanted to be.

After dinner, they decided to take a walk by the waterfront. The sky was just beginning to darken, and the stars were coming out. Lena felt Nicolas take her hand in his, and she leaned into him, enjoying the moment.

As they walked, Lena felt a growing sense of closeness with Nicolas. He was kind and intelligent, and she found herself drawn to him in a way she hadn't expected. They talked about their hopes and dreams, and Lena felt herself opening up to him in a way she never had with anyone else.

It was a beautiful night, and Lena knew that she was having more than just a good time with Nicolas. There was something deeper and more meaningful between them, a connection that went beyond physical attraction. As they walked back to their cars at the end of the night, Lena felt a sense of contentment that she hadn't felt in a long time. This was the beginning of something special, and she was excited to see where it could go.
The Danvers sisters tease Lena about her date.

The next day, Lena met up with Alex and Kara at the DEO, where they were both working. As soon as Lena walked in, she knew she was in for it.

"Hey there, lovebirds," Kara teased, grinning from ear to ear.

Lena rolled her eyes but couldn't help but smile at her friend's enthusiasm. "Don't start, Kara," she warned, knowing exactly where this was going.

"I have no idea what you're talking about," Alex said, raising an eyebrow.

Lena just shook her head and sat down next to them. "Okay, let's just get to work," she said, trying to steer the conversation away from her date with Nicolas.

But it was too late. Alex had pulled out her phone and was scrolling through the group chat they had together. "So, Nicolas, huh?" she said, smirking at Lena.

Lena groaned. "Yes, Nicolas," she said, rolling her eyes. "Can we please talk about something else?"

But Kara wasn't about to let it go. "Come on, Lena," she said. "Spill. Did you have a good time with him?"

Lena couldn't help but smile at the thought of their date. "Yes, I did," she said. "We had a great time."

"I bet you did," Alex said, smirking. "So, when are you seeing him again?"

Lena shook her head in disbelief. "You two are impossible," she said, laughing. "But to answer your question, I don't know. We haven't set a date yet."

"You should do it soon," Kara said, looking serious. "Nicolas seems like a great guy."

Lena nodded, feeling a sense of gratitude for her friends. They may tease her relentlessly, but they were always there for her when she needed them.

And as she thought back to her date with Nicolas, she realized that he might just be the great guy they were all hoping for. With a smile on her face, Lena turned back to her work, grateful for the support and love of her friends.
During this teasing, NIcolas send a message to Lena "Had a great time with you, hope you're having a good day. With a bit of luck i'll see you soon. Nicolas"

Lena's phone buzzed with a message from Nicolas, and she tried to hide her smile from Alex and Kara as she read it.

"What's he saying?" Alex asked, grinning.

Lena just shook her head, not wanting to give them any more ammunition. "Just something about having a good time," she said, trying to play it cool.

Kara leaned over to peek at her phone. "Aww, he's so sweet," she said, reading the message. "You guys are adorable."

Lena blushed, feeling a sense of embarrassment at her friends' teasing. But she couldn't deny the fact that she was enjoying Nicolas's company, and she found herself looking forward to their next date.

"Okay, okay, that's enough," she said, finally putting her foot down. "Can we please talk about something else now?"

Alex and Kara finally relented, but Lena could tell that they were still grinning at her. She just sighed and turned back to her work, feeling grateful for the distraction of her job.

As the day went on, Lena kept thinking about Nicolas and their date. He seemed like such a genuine guy, so different from anyone else she'd ever dated. She found herself hoping that they could continue to see each other, and maybe even build something more meaningful.

With a smile on her face, Lena replied to Nicolas's message, telling him that she had a great time too, and that she hoped they could see each other again soon. And as she hit send, she felt a sense of excitement for what the future might hold.
Nicolas is once again delivering a package to Lena.

A few days had passed since Lena's date with Nicolas, and she found herself looking forward to seeing him again. She was working at L-Corp when she heard a knock on her office door.

"Come in," she called out, expecting to see one of her employees.

But instead, it was Nicolas who walked in, a package in his arms.

"Nicolas!" Lena said, surprised but also pleased to see him. "What are you doing here?"

"I have a delivery for you," he said, holding out the package.

Lena took it from him, her fingers brushing against his for just a moment. It was a small gesture, but it sent a shiver down her spine.

"Thank you," she said, setting the package down on her desk. "How have you been?"

"I've been good, thanks," he replied, looking a bit nervous. "How about you?"

"I've been good too," Lena said, smiling at him. "It's nice to see you again."

Nicolas smiled back, but there was a hint of shyness in his expression. "I'm glad to hear that," he said. "Well, I should probably get going. It was nice to see you."

"You too," Lena said, walking him to the door. "Take care, Nicolas."

As he left, Lena found herself feeling a sense of disappointment that their interaction had been so brief. But at the same time, she felt a sense of excitement at the thought of seeing him again. And as she opened the package he'd delivered, she couldn't help but smile at the thought of Nicolas and their growing relationship.
Delivery for Lena again. Flowers sends from Nicolas. Alex & Kara are there to witness it, and tease Lena afterward.

A few days later, Lena was working at L-Corp when a delivery arrived with a bouquet of flowers. She took the card from the deliveryman and saw that they were from Nicolas.

"Wow," she said, running her fingers over the petals. "These are beautiful."

"What's going on?" Alex asked, walking into the room with Kara.

Lena looked up, feeling a bit embarrassed. "It's just flowers from Nicolas," she said, trying to play it cool.

"Nicolas?" Kara asked, a mischievous glint in her eye. "As in your new man friend?"

Lena groaned, knowing where this was going. "Yes, him," she said, rolling her eyes.

Alex looked over her shoulder at the card. "Oh, it says 'Just thinking of you'," she said, smirking.

Lena felt her cheeks turning pink. "Can we please not make a big deal out of this?" she said, feeling self-conscious.

But Kara was already grinning from ear to ear. "Lena's got a boyfriend," she teased, dancing around the room.

Lena just shook her head, feeling a mix of annoyance and amusement. While she didn't love being the center of attention, she couldn't deny the fact that she was enjoying getting to know Nicolas. He was sweet and thoughtful in a way that she hadn't experienced before, and she found herself looking forward to each new interaction with him.

As Alex and Kara continued to tease her, Lena couldn't help but smile to herself. While she didn't know what the future held for her and Nicolas, she was excited to find out. And for now, she was content just to enjoy the ride.
Nicolas is the one to ask Lena for their second date this time. Hoping for a quiet evening, a walk in the park, a nice diner.

Lena had been thinking about Nicolas a lot since their last interaction, which was the delivery of flowers he had sent her. She was pleasantly surprised when her phone rang, and it was a call from him.

"Hello, Nicolas," she said with a smile on her face.

"Hi, Lena," Nicolas replied. "I was wondering if you're free this weekend?"

"I should be," Lena replied. "What do you have in mind?"

"Well, I thought we could take a walk in the park and maybe have dinner together," Nicolas said. "I know a great spot not too far from here."

Lena could feel her stomach fluttering with excitement at the thought of seeing him again. "That sounds lovely," she said. "What time?"

"How about we meet at the park at around 6 pm?" Nicolas suggested.

"Perfect," Lena said. "I'll see you then."

As she hung up the phone, Lena felt a sense of anticipation building in her chest. She was quickly falling for Nicolas, and she couldn't wait to see where this relationship might go.

The weekend arrived, and Lena found herself standing in the park, waiting for Nicolas to arrive. She was wearing a casual dress and a pair of flats, hoping for a quiet evening under the stars.

As Nicolas walked up to her, Lena felt her heart race. He looked handsome in a button-down shirt and a pair of jeans, and she found herself admiring him as he approached.

"Hey, Lena," he said with a smile.

"Hey," she replied, returning his smile.

And with that, their second date began. They walked through the park, talking and laughing, enjoying the warm summer breeze and each other's company.

As the evening turned to night, they found themselves at a small, cozy restaurant. They shared a meal together, enjoying pasta and wine, with conversation flowing easily between them. Lena found herself drawn to Nicolas's gentle manner and quiet intelligence, and she felt a deeper connection building between them.

As the night drew to a close, Lena found herself hoping for more evenings like this with Nicolas. With a smile on her face, she said goodnight to him, feeling grateful for this chance to get to know him better. And as she walked away, she knew that this was just the beginning of something special.
"I had a loveny evening Lena, thank you for this. Take care. Have a good Night. Nicolas"

Lena's phone buzzed with a message from Nicolas as she walked home from their second date. As she read the text, a smile spread across her face.

"I had a lovely evening too. Thank you for suggesting it, Nicolas. Take care, and have a good night as well. - Lena"

Lena paused for a moment, staring at her phone with a sense of wonder. For the first time in a long while, she felt something fluttering in her chest. Nicolas was unlike anyone she had ever dated before—he was kind, thoughtful, and genuine. He seemed to care about her well-being, and she found herself drawn to him in ways that she couldn't fully explain.

As she continued walking, her mind kept wandering back to their conversation, the warmth of his hand as he held the door open for her, and the way he smiled when they shared inside jokes. Lena felt a sense of excitement and anticipation for what was to come, and she couldn't wait to see what the future had in store for her and Nicolas.
An attack on Nicolas & Lena third date interrupt it. Kara, in her supergirl identity, save the day.

On their third date, Lena and Nicolas had decided to try something different. Instead of their usual dinner and walk in the park, they had planned a night out to watch a movie at a theater.

As they walked out of the theater after the movie, Lena noticed that Nicolas looked a little shaken. "Is everything okay?" she asked, concerned.

"Yeah, I'm fine," Nicolas said, although he still looked a little on edge. "It was a good movie, but it got a little intense at times."

Just as they were about to head to the parking lot, they heard a commotion coming from nearby. Suddenly, a group of men appeared, surrounding them both.

"Give us all your money and valuables," one of them said menacingly.

Lena could feel her heart racing as she realized what was happening. Nicolas looked pale with fear, suddenly finding himself in a life-threatening situation he never expected. Lena could see that the attackers were heavily armed and did not think twice about hurting
